channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Louisiana's 2020 Teacher of the Year is trying to block the state's Ten Commandments law
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana's 2020 Teacher of the Year has filed a lawsuit against the state challenging a new law that requires the Ten Commandments to be displayed in every public school classroom by Jan. 1. It's the second lawsuit filed against the law, which was enacted in June.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Immigration, oil and gas jobs hot issues in volatile House race
item.description: Border Report is featuring stories on closely contested House of Representatives races in border districts that could determine which party controls the lower chamber of Congress for the next two years.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Fallout from 2023 Hamas attack lingers as Jews worldwide prepare for a holiday meant for joy
item.description: Customarily, Simchat Torah is one of the most joyous days of the Jewish calendar, highlighted by exuberant dancing around a Torah.
item.dc:creator: David Crary, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump hurls string of insults at Harris including racist trope against Black people
item.description: Trump signaled he will lean harder into disparaging Harris during the remaining two weeks before Election Day, despite the urging of allies who have repeatedly suggested he should steer clear of personal attacks including references to her race and gender.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Illinois jail continues allowing detainees to vote in presidential elections, other local races
item.description: The Cook County Jail in Chicago is teaching civics classes to those detained. They then have a chance to study their options and vote from the jail's chapel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Young women lean Democratic. Now the election may hinge on Harris' ability to run up the score
item.description: This year's presidential election could see a historic gender gap as young women line up behind Democrat Kamala Harris' bid to be the first female president.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein And Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Carolina to build first monument to an African American. Meet Robert Smalls
item.description: South Carolina is preparing to put up the first individual statue of an African American on its Statehouse lawn.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Want to be a Power Ranger? Original costumes, props up for auction
item.description: It's been over 30 years since the original Mighty Morphin Power Rangers television series aired in the United States, and for the kids who grew up watching the Power Rangers fight evil in Angel Grove, California, their chance to become one of them is now closer than ever.
item.dc:creator: Marco Rosas, Fox 5

item.title: Missouri woman finds trunk full of stolen campaign signs with help of Apple AirTag
item.description: "I expected to find the AirTag, but not 59 signs. It was kind of like finding a dead body," the Missouri woman said.
item.dc:creator: Parker Padgett, NewsNation

item.title: Missouri woman finds trunk full of stolen campaign signs with help of Apple AirTag
item.description: "I expected to find the AirTag, but not 59 signs. It was kind of like finding a dead body," the Missouri woman said.
item.dc:creator: Parker Padgett, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Severe E. coli outbreak linked to McDonald's quarter pounders
item.description: The CDC has issued a food safety alert for an outbreak of E. coli infections linked to McDonald's quarter pounders, with 49 people from ten states affected and one death reported.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Trump selling 'MAGADonald's' shirts after drive-thru appearance
item.description: After Trump’s visit to the Bucks County franchise, McDonald’s sought to emphasize its political neutrality and said it does not endorse political candidates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Grieving families of seniors killed when a dock collapsed say the tragedy was preventable
item.description: JACKSONVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Regina Brinson heard a crack before the metal walkway gave way beneath her feet, plunging her into the water beneath the state-operated ferry dock on Georgia's Sapelo Island.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge orders Giuliani to hand over assets to Georgia election workers
item.description: Rudy Giuliani must turn over much of his property — his apartment in New York, the city that made him mayor, and other prized possessions — to two Georgia election workers who have sought for months to collect their massive defamation judgment, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KRON4

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: Police said they haven't yet confirmed the cause and manner of death of the woman, who worked at the store, nor has her name been released.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, NewsNation

item.title: Meta again testing facial recognition software to fight 'celeb bait' scams
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, is testing facial recognition software to combat “celeb bait” scams on its platforms as well as to allow users to more easily regain access to their accounts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Trump makes more debunked claims about FEMA as he visits storm damaged North Carolina
item.description: Trump was asked whether it was helpful to criticize hurricane relief workers after the Federal Emergency Management Agency recently paused work in the area because of reports they could be targeted by militia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Disgraced Hollywood producer Harvey Weinstein diagnosed with bone cancer: Reports
item.description: Disgraced movie producer Harvey Weinstein has been diagnosed with bone cancer, according to reports. The infamous Hollywood mogul’s diagnosis of chronic myeloid leukemia was first reported by NBC News on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Harris speaks about creating economic opportunity for Latino men; Trump airs familiar grievances
item.description: Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris says she would work to bring more funds to community banks to help Latino men secure small business loans, while Republican Donald Trump’s economic roundtable aimed at Latino voters devolved Tuesday into a tirade of insults against his opponent.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Asian American boba brand finds opportunity after Simu Liu sparks cultural appropriation debate
item.description: Olivia Chen and Pauline Ang, friends and business partners on boba milk tea brand Twrl, have tried three times to get on “Shark Tank,” the ABC reality show where up-and-coming entrepreneurs try to woo big-name backers.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NFL stadiums could lose $11 billion from climate threats by 2050: report
item.description: “This report is a wake-up call for the NFL, stadium owners, and local governments,” said Climate X CEO Lukky Ahmed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Delta crew member reportedly made Marine remove 'threatening' shirt
item.description: A Marine Corps veteran from the San Francisco Bay Area was temporarily removed from a Delta flight on Wednesday by a crew member who deemed her T-shirt to be “threatening,” according to a local report.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Pinellas County man recovering after near-death experience with flesh-eating bacteria
item.description: A Pinellas County man is sharing his near death experience after contracting Vibrio Vulnificus, a flesh-eating bacteria. This comes as the Florida Department of Health is warning people to avoid floodwaters, as cases of the infection continue to rise after back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: PHOTOS: White House public tour gets makeover with 'reimagined' visitor experience
item.description: The White House public tour is getting an extreme makeover, with new digital exhibits, an expanded route and more access to rooms poised to be part of the "reimagined" experience for visitors.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's says it doesn't endorse political candidates after Trump visit
item.description: McDonald's is emphasizing its political neutrality after former President Donald Trump spent some time behind the counter at one of its Pennsylvania locations.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, Fox 5

item.title: Central Park Five sue Donald Trump for jogger case remarks made at presidential debate
item.description: With Election Day two weeks away, the group accused the former president of making “false and defamatory statements.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Paul Di'Anno, former Iron Maiden lead singer, dies at 66
item.description: Former Iron Maiden singer Paul Andrews, better known by his stage name Paul Di'Anno, has died at the age of 66.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KRON4

item.title: Former Ohio officer stands trial in the 2020 shooting death of Andre Hill
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — A white former Ohio police officer has gone on trial Monday in the killing of Andre Hill, a Black man who was shot as he emerged from a garage holding a cellphone nearly four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House proposes insurers cover cost of over-the-counter birth control
item.description: The White House on Monday announced a proposal that would require insurers to cover the cost of forms of contraception like over-the-counter birth control pills and condoms under the Affordable Care Act.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Still using 'LOL'? That could mean you are getting old
item.description: As time has seen several follow-up iterations, including ROFL and LMAO, the original "laugh out loud" acronym is a telltale sign that who you are talking to may be a millennial.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, Fox 5

item.title: This $600 robot litter box is life changing
item.description: Cats are great companions, but scooping out their litter box isn't exactly a chore you look forward to. I've long been curious about automated litter boxes that promise to do the dirty work.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Supreme Court turns away challenge to consumer safety agency’s structure
item.description: The Supreme Court announced Monday it won’t take up a case that could have delivered another blow to federal agencies. Two conservative groups sought to challenge the structure of the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), which oversees making sure the country’s consumer products are safe.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Over 1 million voters cast early ballots in North Carolina
item.description: Nearly 1.01 million North Carolinians have already voted in the November general election, state officials reported on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, ABC 4
